acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apply_event is deterministic and idempotent on repeated events (tests).
- [ ] Note id collision with different content is detected and surfaced.
- [ ] ApplyOutcome reports changes used by indexing and checkpoint scheduling.
description:
**Problem**
The daemon mutates CanonicalState directly, so apply semantics are scattered and can diverge across mutation and replication paths. We need a single pure apply_event path that deterministically maps EventBody deltas to CRDT joins.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.8 (deterministic apply), 2.4 (TxnDeltaV1), 8.3 (apply pipeline)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (TxnOpV1, TxnDeltaV1)

**Files:** src/core/apply.rs (new), src/core/state.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/core/dep.rs, src/core/tombstone.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add core::apply_event(&mut CanonicalState, &EventBody) -> ApplyOutcome as a pure function with no I/O.
- Apply TxnDeltaV1 ops in canonical op key order so repeated application is deterministic.
- Use existing CRDT joins (Lww, Tombstone::join, DepEdge::join) and preserve tombstone resurrection rules.
- Detect note id collisions with mismatched content as corruption (return error), and treat duplicate note append as idempotent.
- Return ApplyOutcome with changed bead ids, dep keys, and note ids to drive indexing and checkpoint dirtiness.
title: Phase 2: core apply_event for EventBody

description:
**Problem**
Config is currently minimal (`src/config.rs` only has auto_upgrade + limits). REALTIME_PLAN ôÏ15.2 requires replication/checkpoint/namespace defaults, and the project needs a maintainable, central config system. We also want repoãlevel `beads.toml` (plus user config) with clear precedence and env overrides.

**Design**
- Introduce a dedicated config module (e.g., `src/config/mod.rs` with submodules `schema.rs`, `load.rs`, `merge.rs`).
- Define layered config types:
  - `UserConfig` (global defaults, stored at `~/.config/beads-rs/config.toml`).
  - `RepoConfig` (repoãlevel `beads.toml`).
  - `EffectiveConfig` (merged result used by CLI/daemon).
- Keep storeãlocal files (`namespaces.toml`, `replicas.toml`, store meta) as authoritative for store identity/policy, but allow repo config to supply defaults when those files are absent.
- Precedence: env vars > repo config > user config > defaults.
- Provide helper to locate repo root and load `beads.toml`.

**Config schema sketch**
- `[defaults]` (namespace, durability, actor?)
- `[replication]` (listen_addr, max_connections, peers, timeouts)
- `[checkpoint.groups.<name>]` (namespaces, git_ref, writers, debounce/max_interval/max_events, durable_copy_via_git)
- `[namespaces.<name>]` (NamespacePolicy defaults if store config missing)
- `[limits]` (existing Limits override)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Central config module with clear layering/merge logic and tests.
- [ ] `beads.toml` supported at repo root; user config still supported.
- [ ] Env overrides are preserved.
- [ ] CLI + daemon use `EffectiveConfig` instead of adãhoc reads where applicable.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Env var audit: map to config where appropriate. Recommend: BD_ACTOR -> defaults.actor (env remains override); BD_REPL_LISTEN_ADDR -> replication.listen_addr; BD_REPL_MAX_CONNECTIONS -> replication.max_connections; BD_LOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS -> timeouts.git_load_secs (or similar). Keep env-only: BD_REMOTE_URL, BD_STORE_ID (debug overrides); BD_DATA_DIR/BD_CONFIG_DIR/BD_WAL_DIR; LOG; BD_NO_AUTO_UPGRADE (override); BD_UPGRADE_* (test/upgrade harness); BD_TEST_WAL_HANG_* (slow-tests). BD_TOMBSTONE_TTL_MS is legacy tombstone GC for git sync; leave env-only or add to a legacy config section if we intentionally support it.

title: Layered config (user+repo beads.toml) with central module
cargo test fails in src/daemon/ipc.rs tests: missing BeadId import for Query::Show/Deps

description:
**Problem**
After the daemon runtime moves to beads-daemon, \`beads-rs\` should be a thin binary facade (~5k lines). But it currently has \`pub mod daemon\` and a massive \`pub use crate::core::{...}\` re-export list that exposes hundreds of internal types. This makes beads-rs look like an API when it's really just a binary crate.

**Design**
1. Remove \`pub mod daemon\` entirely from \`beads-rs/src/lib.rs\` (or feature-gate behind \`daemon-compat\` for transition)
2. Remove the massive crate-root \`pub use crate::core::{...}\` re-export list ã consumers should import from \`beads_core\` directly
3. Make beads-rs export only what the binary entrypoint needs:
   - \`run_cli_entrypoint()\`
   - \`run_daemon_command()\`
   - Telemetry init

After this change, \`beads-rs\` contains:
- \`bin/main.rs\` ã entrypoint (~100 lines)
- \`cli/\` ã thin host adapter (~500 lines)
- \`config/\` ã config loading/merging (~1.3k lines)
- \`compat/\` ã go migration/export (~1k lines)
- \`lib.rs\` ã slim exports + telemetry init
- \`paths.rs\`, \`telemetry.rs\`, \`error.rs\` ã utilities

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No \`pub mod daemon\` in \`beads-rs/src/lib.rs\` (or feature-gated)
- [ ] No massive \`pub use crate::core::{...}\` re-export block
- [ ] \`beads-rs\` public surface is: \`run_cli_entrypoint\`, \`run_daemon_command\`, telemetry types
- [ ] beads-rs source (excluding tests) is <6k lines
- [ ] No external crate depends on \`beads_rs::daemon::*\` or \`beads_rs::core::*\` paths
- [ ] \`cargo check --workspace\` passes
- [ ] \`cargo test --workspace\` passes

**Files:** \`crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs\`, \`crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/\` (delete entirely)
title: Narrow beads-rs public exports ã remove pub mod daemon, slim facade

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `beads-cli` owns full CLI command parse/dispatch/handler implementation.
- [ ] `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/*` is removed or reduced to explicit compat shims with a removal plan.
- [ ] `bd` behavior is preserved: existing critical-path/integration tests pass without output or semantic regressions.
- [ ] Dependency DAG policy in `docs/CRATE_DAG.md` remains satisfied (no forbidden edges introduced).
- [ ] Verification gate passes: `cargo fmt --all`, `just dylint`, `cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo test --features slow-tests`.
- [ ] Architecture closeout doc captures final ownership map and migration deltas.
description:
**Problem**
The CLI/daemon split is still in a hybrid state in the working copy: part of the command surface has moved to `beads-cli`, while substantial parse/dispatch/handler ownership remains in `beads-rs`. This increases maintenance cost and makes crate boundaries harder to reason about.

**Goal**
Complete the move so `beads-cli` owns the full CLI command surface and `beads-rs` remains a thin orchestration/compat layer only.

**Files**
- crates/beads-cli/**
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/**
- crates/beads-rs/src/bin/main.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs
- crates/beads-rs/tests/**
- docs/architecture/**
- docs/CRATE_DAG.md
design:
1. Inventory remaining command handlers and top-level CLI wiring still owned by `beads-rs`.
2. Move pure IPC/read-only command paths first to minimize risk.
3. For host-coupled commands (e.g. migrate/store/upgrade), expose typed backend seams and migrate without introducing `beads-cli -> beads-rs` or `beads-cli -> beads-daemon` forbidden edges.
4. Move top-level parse/dispatch/runtime wiring into `beads-cli`, then keep `beads-rs` entrypoint as thin call-through shim.
5. Rewire integration tests/fixtures to assert behavior parity (output shape, exit codes, daemon interactions).
6. Keep boundary policy enforced with `just dylint` and update closeout docs.
title: Finish full CLI ownership move from beads-rs to beads-cli
Move more CLI validation into core types (parse helpers)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Unit test: same replica adds two values, remove one -> other survives.
- [ ] Unit test: apply_remove with ctx containing dot for value A does NOT remove value B from same replica.
- [ ] Join test: a has value A, b removes B and advances ctx; join preserves A.
- [ ] Join test: concurrent add vs remove (ctx not covering new dot) is add-wins.
- [ ] Serialization roundtrip preserves DVV semantics (encode/decode for DVV if format changed).
description:
**Problem**
OrSet uses Dvv as max-per-replica and prunes any dot dominated by cc. A remove of one value advances cc and incorrectly removes unrelated values added earlier by the same replica. This breaks labels/deps semantics (remove one label can erase others).

**Files**: src/core/orset.rs, src/core/state.rs, src/core/event.rs, src/git/wire.rs, src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs
design:
**Design**
Implement correct ORSWOT removal semantics by making Dvv a dotted version vector (max + dot exceptions) or equivalent tombstone mechanism so removal can target specific dots without wiping earlier dots from the same replica.

Implementation sketch:
- Extend Dvv to include an explicit dot set (exception set).
- dominates(dot) returns true if dot.counter <= max[replica] OR dot in dots.
- merge/join merges max and dot sets and compacts dots covered by max.
- apply_remove(value, ctx) removes dots for that value dominated by ctx and merges ctx into cc (without over-approximating unrelated dots).
- Update encode/decode for DVV if representation changes (wire + git + WAL).
- Add normalization to drop empty entries and resolve collisions deterministically after join.

Testing should focus on remove targeting only intended dots and add-wins behavior under concurrency.
title: OR-Set DVV pruning removes unrelated values (fix ORSWOT semantics)

acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- plan cannot be called without a validated StampedContext (compile-time change).
- Mismatched actor/stamp is impossible to represent or yields a clear OpError at construction.
- Tests cover mismatch rejection and valid stamping.
description:
Problem
- MutationEngine::plan accepts a Stamp separate from MutationContext.actor_id and never verifies they match.
- A caller can pass mismatched actor/HLc stamp, yielding events whose HLC actor differs from the request actor.
- This violates causality invariants and is not caught by the compiler.

Impact
- Incorrect event authorship and HLC ordering; difficult-to-debug replication issues.
- Violates "types should tell the truth"; the API implies any Stamp is valid.
design:
Design
- Introduce a StampedContext type that can only be constructed from MutationContext + Stamp after verifying stamp.by == ctx.actor_id.
- Change plan signature to accept StampedContext (or accept a stamp builder that derives from ctx).
- Option: have plan compute stamp from ctx + wall clock, and only accept the wall clock inputs, not a raw Stamp.
- Add a debug assertion or validation error if mismatch is detected in legacy call sites.

Scatter fit
- Make the mismatch unrepresentable; no call site can pass a mismatched stamp.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/executor.rs (callers)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core.rs (callers)
title: mutation: stamp must be derived from actor context

description: src/cli/commands/show.rs calls fetch_issue_summary per id for deps/children/related. Use Request::List with Filters::ids to fetch all summaries in one IPC round-trip, then map id->summary.
title: CLI show: batch summaries for related issue lists
